title: Use Problem Details for HTTP API Error Responses
summary: Errors returned by HTTP APIs should use the application/problem+json media type with a body containing type (URI), title, status, detail, and instance fields. Domain-specific extension members are allowed.
rationale: RFC 9457 supersedes RFC 7807 and gives every HTTP API a single, RFC-track error envelope so that cross-vendor tooling and clients can parse failures uniformly.

examples.snippet:
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
Content-Type: application/problem+json

{
  "type": "https://example.com/probs/out-of-credit",
  "title": "You do not have enough credit.",
  "status": 403,
  "detail": "Your current balance is 30, but that costs 50.",
  "instance": "/account/12345/msgs/abc"
}
